symptoms.symptom_name: Medial-malleolar shallow ulcer with venous skin changes, elevation-relieved pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Venous leg ulcer with secondary cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Medial-malleolar ulcer with haemosiderin/lipodermatosclerosis and elevation-relief is characteristic of a venous ulcer.
Arterial ulcers are pulseless and worse on elevation.

symptoms.symptom_name: New tender warm spreading peri-wound erythema + increased malodorous exudate + throbbing pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Venous leg ulcer with secondary cellulitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acute peri-wound cellulitis on a chronic venous ulcer.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Spreading cellulitis / sepsis or deep infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No ascending redness/fever/leg-wide swelling makes systemic infection unlikely now.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude arterial disease before applying compression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Imaging for osteomyelitis if the ulcer is deep/non-healing
